title: Writing - Part 2: Exercise 1
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 220-260 words.

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt: Your college principal has asked you to write a report on the use of digital devices in the classroom. Your report should describe how students currently use devices, evaluate the benefits and drawbacks, and suggest how their use could be better regulated.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Report on the Use of Digital Devices in the Classroom

Introduction
This report examines how students at Westfield College currently use personal digital devices during lessons, assesses the overall impact on learning, and puts forward recommendations for a more structured approach.

Current Usage
Observations across departments reveal that the majority of students use laptops or tablets to take notes and access online resources. However, a significant proportion also use their devices for non-academic purposes such as social media, messaging, and gaming, particularly during longer lectures.

Benefits and Drawbacks
On the positive side, digital devices facilitate instant access to information, enable collaborative document editing, and allow students to organise their notes efficiently. Nonetheless, the potential for distraction is considerable. Research suggests that multitasking with a device significantly reduces retention of information, and the presence of screens can disrupt the focus of neighbouring students as well.

Recommendations
To maximise the educational advantages while minimising disruption, the following measures are proposed:
- Introducing a clear 'device policy' at the start of each module, specifying when devices are permitted.
- Encouraging lecturers to incorporate structured digital activities rather than allowing unrestricted access.
- Designating specific rows or sections of the room as 'device-free' zones for students who prefer them.

Conclusion
Digital devices are a valuable academic tool when used purposefully. A consistent, institution-wide policy would help ensure that technology supports, rather than undermines, the learning environment.

tasks.title: Letter
tasks.prompt: You recently purchased a laptop from an online retailer. The product was faulty and, despite several attempts to resolve the matter, the company has failed to provide a satisfactory solution. Write a letter to the company's customer services director explaining the problem, describing your previous attempts to resolve the issue, and demanding appropriate compensation.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Ms. Hartley,

I am writing to formally escalate an unresolved complaint regarding a laptop I purchased from your website on 14th February (Order No. WEB-7823). Despite three separate attempts to seek a remedy through your standard customer service channels, I have received no satisfactory resolution, and I am now compelled to address this matter directly to your office.

Upon receiving the laptop, I discovered that the screen flickered persistently and the battery failed to charge beyond forty percent. I contacted your helpdesk within twenty-four hours of delivery and was advised to perform a factory reset, which did not resolve either issue. On my second call, I was promised a collection for repair within five working days; no collection was ever arranged. My third attempt, submitted via your online complaints form three weeks ago, has received no response whatsoever.

This level of service is entirely unacceptable, particularly given the price of the product, which exceeded £900. I have been without a functioning laptop for over six weeks, causing significant disruption to my professional commitments.

I am therefore requesting a full refund of the purchase price, including original delivery costs. Should I not receive a satisfactory written response within seven working days of this letter, I will have no alternative but to escalate this matter to the relevant consumer protection authority.

I trust you will treat this complaint with the urgency it deserves.

Yours sincerely,
Patrick Holloway

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt: You have seen the following notice in an international student magazine: 'Have you visited a museum or gallery that left a lasting impression? Write a review telling our readers about the experience, the exhibits, and whether you would recommend it.' Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
A Window into the Past: The National Museum of Civilisations

Nestled in the heart of the capital, the National Museum of Civilisations is an institution that manages to make the distant past feel both immediate and deeply relevant. My recent visit left me with a sense of wonder that few cultural experiences have matched.

The museum's permanent collection spans six millennia of human history, from the earliest Mesopotamian settlements to the complexities of the Renaissance. The centrepiece of the ground floor is a breathtaking reconstruction of a Roman marketplace, complete with authentic artefacts and ambient sound design that transports visitors directly into the bustle of ancient commerce. It is the kind of immersive exhibit that ignites the imagination in a way that a textbook never could.

The upper galleries, dedicated to the medieval period, are equally impressive. The illuminated manuscripts on display are extraordinarily well-preserved, and the accompanying explanatory panels strike an ideal balance between scholarly detail and accessibility for general audiences. I was particularly struck by an interactive exhibit that allowed visitors to attempt transcribing text using a digital quill, providing genuine insight into the patience required of medieval scribes.

My only minor criticism is that the café facilities are somewhat inadequate for the volume of visitors, particularly during peak weekend hours. Nevertheless, this is a trivial inconvenience in the context of such a rich and rewarding experience.

I would wholeheartedly recommend the National Museum of Civilisations to anyone with even a passing interest in history. It is a masterclass in making the past come alive.
